county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Ellis County formed 1849 from Navarro. County seat is Waxahachie. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Waxahachie. North Texas county. Ellis County Museum. Italian immigrant community in Ennis. Cotton farming heritage. Waxahachie has extensive Victorian architecture records. Check Navarro for pre-1849 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Ellis County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1849; County Clerk in Waxahachie holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Ellis County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1849 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Ellis County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1849. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Ellis County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1849. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Ellis County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Ellis County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Ellis County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Ellis County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Ellis County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Ellis County from 1849. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Erath County formed 1856 from Bosque, Coryell. County seat is Stephenville. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Stephenville. Central-west Texas. Erath County Historical Commission. Tarleton State University library. Stephenville was a dairy center. Check Bosque and Coryell for pre-1856 records.

source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Falls County formed 1850 from Limestone, Milam. County seat is Marlin. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Marlin. Central Texas county. Falls County Historical Commission. Significant African American community. Cotton plantation economy. Marlin mineral water spa attracted visitors. Check Limestone and Milam for pre-1850 records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Fannin County formed 1837 from Original Texas county (Red River). County seat is Bonham. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Bonham. Original Texas county (Red River area) — one of the oldest Anglo settlement areas. Fannin County Museum of History. Sam Rayburn Library and Museum in Bonham. Extensive early records from Republic of Texas era. Peters Colony overlap. Bonham area has significant African American history.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Fannin County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1837; County Clerk in Bonham holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Fayette County formed 1837 from Bastrop, Colorado. County seat is La Grange. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in La Grange. Central Texas county — strong Czech and German communities. Fayette Heritage Museum. Fayette County Record newspaper archives. Painted Churches of Schulenburg (Czech heritage). Confederate reunion grounds. Kreische Brewery State Historic Site. Check Bastrop and Colorado for pre-1837 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Fayette County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1837; County Clerk in La Grange holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Fayette County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / TSLAC / Texas Lutheran University archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German immigrant church records including baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Fayette County has significant German settlement heritage. German-language records may require translation. Check local Lutheran, Catholic, and Methodist congregations.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Fisher County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Roby. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Roby. Rolling Plains county organized 1886. Farming and ranching. Fisher County Historical Commission. Check Bexar for pre-organization records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Fort Bend County formed 1837 from Austin, Harrisburg. County seat is Richmond. Gulf Coast region with port, plantation, and petrochemical heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Richmond. Gulf Coast county — Stephen F. Austin's colony. Fort Bend Museum. Major plantation county — extensive enslaved persons records (sugar plantations). Significant African American heritage. Imperial Sugar Company records. Check Austin and Harrisburg for pre-1837 records. Freedmen's communities (Kendleton, Rosenberg area).
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Fort Bend County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1837; County Clerk in Richmond holds deed books
